title: Today's AI Brief: How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment
summary: Pope Leo XIV’s new encyclical on artificial intelligence, Magnifica Humanitas, and open-source software helping robots think.
imageAlt: Image from source article for How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment
imageCaption: How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment
imageSourceTitle: How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. We're looking at some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. The Pope has weighed in on AI with a new encyclical called Magnifica Humanitas, which offers a template for individuals to navigate the AI moment.

Also making headlines is the rise of open-source software in robotics. IEEE Spectrum reports that open-source robotics hardware has been around for a while, but now the focus is shifting to getting robots to think. Open-source AI platforms are emerging, which could help accelerate the development of more sophisticated robots. It's a bit like giving robots a brain transplant – except instead of a brain, they're getting code.

Another item that caught my eye is the ongoing effort to make AI more accessible and transparent. While not directly related to the previous items, it's clear that the AI landscape is evolving rapidly, and there are many different approaches to developing and deploying AI systems.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
